title: contingency-dsl Operant Stateful AST
description: AST schema extension for the operant stateful sub-layer. Schedules whose criteria are computed from runtime state. Additive to schema/operant/ast.schema.json — does not modify existing operant-layer definitions.

PercentileSchedule.description: Percentile schedule (Platt, 1973; Galbicka, 1994). Reinforces responses meeting a specified percentile of the subject's recent response distribution on a given dimension. Criterion = f(response_history), computed at runtime.

pctl_target.description: Response dimension to measure. IRT: inter-response time (Platt, 1973; Kuch & Platt, 1976). latency: response latency (Galbicka, 1988). duration: response duration (Galbicka, 1988). force: response force (Notterman & Mintz, 1965). rate: local response rate (Galbicka, 1994).

pctl_rank.description: Percentile threshold (0–100). Rank=50 is the median. Rank=0 reinforces all responses (CRF equivalent, linter warning). Rank=100 reinforces no responses (EXT equivalent, linter warning).

pctl_window.description: Number of recent responses used to compute the distribution. Default: 20 (Platt, 1973 recommendation). Linter warns if < 5 (PCTL_SMALL_WINDOW) or > 100 (PCTL_LARGE_WINDOW).

pctl_dir.description: Reinforcement direction. 'below': reinforce responses at or below the percentile threshold (e.g., shorter IRTs). 'above': reinforce responses at or above (e.g., longer durations, greater force). Default: 'below'.

AdjParamValue.description: A parameter value for an adjusting schedule. For delay target, time_unit is required. For ratio/amount targets, time_unit is null.

value.description: The numeric value of the parameter. Must be positive (> 0).

time_unit.description: Time unit. Required for delay target (non-null), must be null for ratio/amount targets.

AdjustingSchedule.description: Adjusting schedule (Blough, 1958; Ferster & Skinner, 1957; Mazur, 1987; Richards et al., 1997). A procedural framework in which a schedule parameter is systematically adjusted based on the subject's recent performance. The adjustment direction is determined at runtime; the DSL declares the target, initial value, step size, and optional bounds. Standalone schedule expression (not a modifier).

adj_target.description: What parameter is adjusted. delay: reinforcement delay (Mazur, 1987). ratio: response ratio requirement (Ferster & Skinner, 1957; Barrett, 1976). amount: reinforcer magnitude/quantity (Richards et al., 1997; Bradshaw et al., 2020).

adj_start.description: Initial value of the adjusted parameter. Required.

adj_step.description: Adjustment magnitude per step (additive). Required. The direction (increase or decrease) is determined by the runtime based on the subject's behavior.

adj_min.description: Lower bound on the adjusted parameter. Optional. Null means no lower bound.

adj_max.description: Upper bound on the adjusted parameter. Optional. Null means no upper bound. Linter warns if null for delay target (ADJ_UNBOUNDED_DELAY).

InterlockingSchedule.description: Interlocking schedule (Ferster & Skinner, 1957). A compound schedule in which the ratio requirement decreases linearly as time elapses since the last reinforcement. At each reinforcement, the ratio resets to its initial value. R(t) = max(1, ⌈R0 × (1 − t/T)⌉). Standalone schedule expression (not a modifier). Deterministic time function — the only operant stateful schedule that does not depend on the subject's behavior history.

interlock_R0.description: Initial ratio requirement (R₀). The number of responses required for reinforcement immediately after the previous reinforcement. Positive integer. Linter warns if R0=1 (INTERLOCK_TRIVIAL_RATIO, effectively FT) or R0>500 (INTERLOCK_LARGE_RATIO).

interlock_T.description: Time window (T). When elapsed time since last reinforcement equals T, the ratio requirement reaches 1 (CRF). Positive, time unit required.

value.description: The numeric value of the time window. Must be positive.

CoreStatefulScheduleExpr.description: Union of all operant stateful schedule expressions. Additive to the operant layer's ScheduleExpr oneOf.
